findings.summary: canary asserted a subset of the fresh arm (no init, no short-form version checks) — added; asserted strings verified against src/init.ts and src/cli.ts output shapes

attestation check warns instead of failing, deviating from RFC-029's 'asserts provenance was attached' — accepted: failing post-publish deprecates nothing (publish is irreversible) and attestations can lag propagation; disclosed in the PR body; a hard re-check is owned by the first real run / the ADR-0058 doctor work

workflow_dispatch tag input was interpolated into run scripts of the OIDC-bearing job — inputs now env-routed, VERSION semver-validated (injection strings observed rejected), top-level contents:read default added, tag glob tightened to v[0-9]*

the workflow end-to-end remains unobserved (npmjs trusted-publishing setup is operator-side) — per ADR-0051 the npm channel's delivery status is UNPROVEN until the first green canary run, and the README carries no npm install command until then
